title: Bridge Paper: Inner speech and the neurobiology of psychosis.
author: Frontier Science / Hermes T2

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Daimon** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge thesis:** Voice-hearing — auditory verbal hallucinations on a continuum, inner speech misattribution, source monitoring, corollary discharge.
**Phase context:** auditory verbal hallucination, hallucination proneness, voice hearing, inner speech, corollary discharge, source monitoring

Paper: Inner speech and the neurobiology of psychosis.
DOI: 10.1016/j.neubiorev.2026.106805
Abstract: Aberrations of inner speech have been linked to psychotic symptoms such as thought insertion and auditory verbal hallucinations. These symptoms may reflect failures of prediction and source monitoring. Normally, efference copies of speech motor commands are sent to auditory cortices and suppressed, helping distinguish self-generated from external input. If suppression malfunctions, predicted auditory input may become perceptually salient. Further, if self-monitoring or error detection-related re
